english: mile, acc. to Cs. = 4000 fathoms, hence a geographical mile; yet there are mentioned dpag-ćén (dpag chen) and dpág-ćùṅ (dpag chung), the latter = 500 fathoms. The word seems altogether to belong more to the phantastic mythical literature, than to common life; so at least in W.
the bustle or tumult of a festival Ld.
n. of a fabulous tree, that grants every wish; acc. to Pth. = tsán-dan-sbrúl-gyi snyíṅ-po (tsan dan sbrul gyi snying po).

witness, both the deponent, and the evidence deposed. Fully authenticated are as yet only: lha dpáṅ-du _dzúg-pa (lha dpang du 'dzug pa) to call a deity for a witness in taking an oath, to appeal to Glr.; also: dpaṅ byéd-pa (dpang byed pa) to bear witness, to attest, v. mna. More conjectural are the meanings of: blo-séms dpáṅ-du _jóg-pa (blo sems dpang du 'jog pa) Glr., or raṅ-séms dpáṅ-du _dzúg-pa (rang sems dpang du 'dzug pa) Mil., to be sincere, to be conscious of speaking the truth; dpáṅ-du _gyúr-ba (dpang du 'gyur ba) to be witness of, to see, to know (cf. spyáṅ-du gyúr-ba (spyang du gyur ba)); bden-dpáṅ (bden dpang) Liś. as explanation of čé-bži (che bzhi), witness or proof for the truth of a thing; *páṅ-po lóg-pa zér-čes* (*pang po log pa zer ches*) W. to give false evidence (Schr. rdzun-dpáṅ (rdzun dpang)). — mi-dpáṅ (mi dpang) (Ld. *pír-paṅ* (*pir pang*)) W., C., is used as syn. to dpáṅ-po (dpang po) (also Schr.), 1. witness. 2. defender, advocate; mi-dpaṅ (mi dpang) (or dpaṅ-po (dpang po)) byéd-pa (byed pa) c. genit. or dat., to defend in a court of justice; (dpáṅ-pos dpóṅ-ba (dpang pos dpong ba) Sch. seems to be unknown and doubtful).
height; dpáṅs-su (dpangs su) in height Samb.; dpaṅs-mtó (dpangs mtho) Lex. high, cf. _páṅs ('pangs). — dpaṅs-tsád (dpangs tshad) great heat Schr. (?).

1. glory, splendour, magnificence, abundance; dpal reg-pa-méd-pa (dpal reg pa med pa) unattainable glory Glr.; yóṅ-tan dú-mai dpal (yon tan du ma'i dpal) splendour of numerous accomplishments; skyéd-pai dpál-la loṅs-spyód-pa (skyed pa'i dpal la longs spyod pa) enjoying the utmost happiness Glr.; frq. as an epithet, or part of the names of deities, e.g. dpal-čén hé-ru-ka (dpal chen he ru ka), and esp. dpal(-ldan)lhá-mo (dpal (-ldan) lha mo), dpal-čén-mo (dpal chen mo), Durga Uma, Kāli, the much adored spouse of Siva; _dod-dgúi dpal ('dod dgu'i dpal) the fulness of all that can be desired Glr.; dpál-gyi dúm-bu (dpal gyi dum bu), श्रीखण्ड, 1. sandal-wood. 2. Cs. a kind of syrup, prepared of bsé-śiṅ (bse shing), used as a purgative. — 2. wealth, abundance, Glr. and elsewh. — 3. welfare, happiness, blessing, _gró-bai ('gro ba'i) of creatures Mil. and elsewh.; kún-gyi dpál-du _gyúr-ba (kun gyi dpal du 'gyur ba) or śár-ba (shar ba) to be (become) the salvation, the saviour of all beings Glr. and elsewh.; dpal skyéd-pa (dpal skyed pa), gžán-gyi (gzhan gyi), ráṅ-gi dpal (rang gi dpal) to work for the elevation of others or for one's own. — 4. nobility, dpál-gyi gnáṅ-ba (dpal gyi gnang ba) privilege of nobility; dpál-gyi gnaṅ-śóg (dpal gyi gnang shog) diploma of nobility, dpál-gyi gnaṅ-śóg-pa (dpal gyi gnang shog pa) one having a diploma of nobility Cs. — dpal-kyád (dpal khyad) Dzl. = dpal 1. — dpal-rtúg (dpal rtug) majesty, full glory Sch. — dpal-ldán (dpal ldan) a man's name (very common). — dpal-pó (dpal po) an illustrious man, dpál-mo (dpal mo) an illustrious woman Cs. — dpal(-gyi)-béu (dpal (-gyi) be'u) is said to denote the figure [knot symbol] Glr. — dpal-byéu (dpal bye'u) glow-worm Sch. — dpal-byór (dpal 'byor) 1. glory, wealth, magnificence, as a man's possession. 2. W. strawberry; 3. a man's name (very common).

1 host, great number, _baṅs tams-čád-kyi ('bangs thams cad kyi) Dzl.; esp. of soldiers. — 2. troops, army, dpúṅ bži (dpung bzhi) the four species of troops: rtai (rta'i), gláṅ-po-čei (glang po che'i), śíṅ-rtai (shing rta'i), and rkáṅ-táṅ-gi dpúṅ (rkang thang gi dpung) (or dpúṅ(-bu)-čúṅ (dpung (-bu) chung)); dpúṅ-(gi) tsógs (dpung (gi) tshogs), dmag-dpúṅ (dmag dpung), army frq.; dgra-dpúṅ (dgra dpung) hostile army. — 3. (auxiliaries?), help, assistance, *púṅ-la táṅ-če* (*pung la tang che*) W. to send assistance. — dpúṅ-grógs (dpung grogs), -rogs (-rogs), helper. — dpúṅ-(gi) gnyen (dpung (gi) gnyen) friend, protector, defender, assistant, frq. — *puṅ-nón* (*pung non*) W. reinforcement.

1. shoulder, dpúṅ-pa steṅ (dpung pa steng) on the shoulder Glr.; dpúṅ-pa daṅ dpyi gnyis (dpung pa dang dpyi gnyis) both the shoulders and hips S.g.; upper arm, dpuṅ-pa-rkáṅ (dpung pa rkang) upper arm-bone; dpuṅ júm-pa (dpung 'jum pa) Sch. to contract the arm(?); dpuṅ-pa-lág (dpung pa lag) upper and lower arm Cs.; dpuṅ-pa-rgyán (dpung pa rgyan) an ornament for the arm Cs. — 2. sleeve, gos dpúṅ-pa-čan (gos dpung pa can) a garment
